raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":0.7,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Improving the draft centrally includes sharpening the contribution and methodological framing above the sentence level.","R1.2":"The request implies responsibility for checking whether the methodological setup, evaluation, and conclusion follow coherently.","R1.3":"Because the draft reports performance figures and comparative claims, improvement includes aligning those claims with the reported evidence.","R1.5":"The draft may need section- or paragraph-level reordering to reduce redundancy and improve the narrative arc.","R1.6":"Writing polish is load-bearing because the delegation explicitly asks to improve a prose draft with evident repetition and flow issues.","RX.1":"Any projection of improvement must preserve uncertainty about what can be inferred from the excerpt alone.","RX.2":"The improvement responsibility includes avoiding stronger claims than the reported evaluation supports.","RX.3":"The agent must stay within improving the draft rather than executing research, inventing results, or changing the artifact here.","RX.4":"The projection must avoid encouraging misleading scientific claims or unsupported methodological authority.","RX.5":"Traceability is required so changes or judgments remain grounded in the provided artifact rather than unstated sources."}}

rationale.R1.1: Improving the draft centrally includes sharpening the contribution and methodological framing above the sentence level.
rationale.R1.2: The request implies responsibility for checking whether the methodological setup, evaluation, and conclusion follow coherently.
rationale.R1.3: Because the draft reports performance figures and comparative claims, improvement includes aligning those claims with the reported evidence.
rationale.R1.5: The draft may need section- or paragraph-level reordering to reduce redundancy and improve the narrative arc.
rationale.R1.6: Writing polish is load-bearing because the delegation explicitly asks to improve a prose draft with evident repetition and flow issues.
rationale.RX.1: Any projection of improvement must preserve uncertainty about what can be inferred from the excerpt alone.
rationale.RX.2: The improvement responsibility includes avoiding stronger claims than the reported evaluation supports.
rationale.RX.3: The agent must stay within improving the draft rather than executing research, inventing results, or changing the artifact here.
rationale.RX.4: The projection must avoid encouraging misleading scientific claims or unsupported methodological authority.
rationale.RX.5: Traceability is required so changes or judgments remain grounded in the provided artifact rather than unstated sources.
